nodes.description: Promotes bud dormancy; treatment reduces tillering.
Relays phosphate signal from HK4 to type-B RR21.
Ammonium-specific N species; activates PIN9 auxin efflux carrier.
Apical auxin maintains bud dormancy via IPT repression + SL-biosynthesis activation + TB1 activation.
BR-stabilised TF; recruits D53 to TB1 promoter for additional repression.
Stabilises BZR1 (which complexes with D53 on TB1 promoter) and activates DLT.
Morning-peak clock component; activates TB1/D14/D10/IPA1; mutually inhibits PRR1.
Primary CK-degrading enzyme; gn1a LOF accumulates CK and increases tillers.
CK oxidase; SL-responsive via D53 de-repression; couples SL -> CK depletion.
Promotes tiller bud outgrowth; balance of IPT/LOG synthesis vs CKX degradation.
Carotenoid cleavage dioxygenase 8; converts 9-cis-beta-apo-10'-carotenal to carlactone.
SL receptor; binds GR24/endogenous SL, then co-inhibits D53 with D3.
CCD7; cleaves 9-cis-beta-carotene upstream of D10.
First committed step of SL biosynthesis; converts all-trans to 9-cis beta-carotene.
Constitutive F-box; co-inhibitor of D53 together with ligand-bound D14.
Double-brake repressor of IPA1 and MOC1; SL-induced ubiquitin-proteasome turnover.
Positive regulator of tillering; BR-activated; repressed by TCP19.
Binds DBS motif in CKX2 promoter and activates its transcription.
Required for photosynthesis-derived sucrose accumulation in shoot base; sucrose -> NGR5.
Removes polar auxin source; releases axillary buds.
CLAVATA1-like kinase; specifically required for tiller bud elongation (fon1 forms buds but fails to elongate).
CLV3-like peptide; restricts TAB1/MOC3 expression domain.
GA biosynthesis enzyme; sd1 (null) is the Green Revolution allele; reduced GA -> increased tillers.
Zn-finger GATA TF; represses OsTCP19 (relieves DLT) and balances N-uptake with tiller productivity.
Degrades SLR1 and NGR5 via GID1; net GA effect is anti-tillering.
Primary CK receptor for tillering; phosphorylates AHP.
SBP-box TF; activates TB1/LOG/PIN1b; repressed by D53, miR156, IPI1, SHI1, NGR5.
RING-type E3 ubiquitin ligase; ubiquitinates IPA1 (K48 chains in panicles).
Rate-limiting CK biosynthesis enzymes (OsIPT4/OsIPT7 dominant in shoots).
bHLH TF essential for AM initiation; lax1 + downstream axillary meristem loss.
N-induced TF that directly represses OsTCP19.
Converts CK nucleotides to active free bases.
Entrains circadian CCA1 expression.
MADS-box TF; binds CArG motif in D14 promoter and represses D14 transcription.
Generic collapsed MAX1 paralog family; converts carlactone to canonical SLs.
N-fertilisation-inducible MAX1-like; promoter mutations increase tillers under low N.
Master activator of axillary meristem initiation; stabilised by SLR1; degraded by TAD1/TE APC/C.
Sucrose biosynthesis; moc2 shows monoculm-like tillering defect.
WUSCHEL homolog required for AM initiation; cooperates with MOC1 to activate FON1.
Recruits PRC2 to TB1 and IPA1 promoters for H3K27me3 repression; N- and sucrose-activated, GA-degraded.
Indica/japonica natural variation in N-responsive phosphorylation of NGR5.
Nitrate + ammonium pool; activates NGR5 (NRT1.1B -> PRC2) and LBD37 arms, represses SL biosynthesis genes.
PIN1 paralog; KO increases tillers; IPA1-activated.
Ammonium-specifically-induced PIN; OE increases tillers.
Evening-peak clock component; mutually inhibits CCA1.
P sufficiency represses SL biosynthesis; P starvation elevates D27/D10/D17 and SL.
Regulates PRR1 evening peak in rice clock.
DNA-binding TF; binds CK-response elements in IPA1 promoter.
CK-induced negative feedback regulators that dampen CK signalling.
Physically binds IPA1 and represses its transcriptional activation.
Growth-repressing DELLA; stabilises MOC1 and NGR5; degraded upon GA-GID1 perception.
Branching-suppressing hormone synthesised via D27/D17/D10/MAX1 pathway; perceived by D14.
Photosynthetic sugar; stabilises D53, activates NGR5, represses CCA1, produces T6P.
Upstream of D27 in carotenoid pathway; supplies precursors for both SL and ABA.
Sugar-status signal that represses TB1 in buds.
Recruits MOC1 to APC/C for cell-cycle-dependent proteasomal degradation.
First step of tryptophan-dependent IAA biosynthesis.
TCP family branching-repressor TF; integrates IPA1, CK, Auxin, NGR5, T6P signals.
N-responsive TCP TF; represses DLT; N -> LBD37 -| TCP19 relieves DLT repression.
APC/C co-activator that targets MOC1 for ubiquitin-proteasome degradation.
Final phenotype: number of rice tillers produced (per plant, per unit area).
Chromatin-interacting factor; binds and represses OsTB1 promoter (Yoon 2019).
Rate-limiting step of IAA biosynthesis (IPA -> IAA).
Cleaves IPA1/OsSPL14 mRNA; juvenile-phase high expression.
Cleaves OsMADS57 mRNA; miR444 OE reduces tillers via MADS57 loss.

edges.mechanism: SL binds D14 alpha/beta hydrolase, induces conformational change and formation of D14-D3-D53 complex

evidence.title: DWARF 53 acts as a repressor of strigolactone signalling in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: D53 was shown to interact with D14 by pull-down assay. Interaction enhanced by GR24 in a dose-dependent manner.
evidence.claim: SL binding activates D14 to form complex with D53

evidence.title: D14-SCFD3-dependent degradation of D53 regulates strigolactone signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: Y2H showed that both D53 and d53 could physically interact with D14 in the presence of GR24.

edges.mechanism: D14 recruits SCF-D3 E3 ligase to ubiquitinate D53 for 26S proteasomal degradation

evidence.title: DWARF 53 acts as a repressor of strigolactone signalling in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: D53 protein levels decreased rapidly upon GR24 treatment in wild-type seedlings. Mutations at Ser-His-Asp catalytic triad could attenuate the SL-induced degradation of D53.
evidence.claim: D14 is essential for SL-induced D53 degradation

edges.mechanism: D3 is the F-box subunit of SCF complex that ubiquitinates D53; required for proteasomal degradation

evidence.title: D14-SCFD3-dependent degradation of D53 regulates strigolactone signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: SLs induce D53 degradation by the proteasome and abrogate its activity in promoting axillary bud outgrowth, in a D14- and D3-dependent manner.
evidence.claim: D3 (F-box) is required for D53 ubiquitination and degradation

edges.mechanism: D53 binds IPA1 via direct protein-protein interaction and represses IPA1 transcriptional activation activity

evidence.title: IPA1 functions as a downstream transcription factor repressed by D53 in strigolactone signaling in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: D53 physically interacts with IPA1 in vivo and in vitro and suppresses the transcriptional activation activity of IPA1.

edges.mechanism: D53 physically interacts with MOC1 to repress tiller bud outgrowth

evidence.title: Strigolactones and BRC1 directly regulate tillering via D53 and MOC1
evidence.evidence_sentence: D53 interacts with MOC1 to repress MOC1 activity in tiller regulation.

edges.mechanism: D53 represses OsCKX9 expression; SL-induced D53 degradation derepresses OsCKX9

evidence.title: Strigolactone promotes cytokinin degradation through transcriptional activation of CYTOKININ OXIDASE/DEHYDROGENASE 9 in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: D53 represses OsCKX9; knockdown of D53 increases OsCKX9 expression.

edges.mechanism: IPA1 directly binds GTAC motif in OsTB1 promoter via SBP domain and activates transcription

evidence.title: Genome-Wide Binding Analysis of the Transcription Activator IDEAL PLANT ARCHITECTURE1 Reveals a Complex Network Regulating Rice Plant Architecture
evidence.evidence_sentence: IPA1 could directly bind to the promoter of rice TEOSINTE BRANCHED1 via GTAC motif binding to suppress rice tillering.

evidence.title: IPA1 functions as a downstream TF repressed by D53
evidence.evidence_sentence: IPA1 directly binds to the promoter of a negative regulator of tiller bud outgrowth, TEOSINTE BRANCHED1 (OsTB1), to suppress rice tillering.

edges.mechanism: IPA1 directly binds GTAC element in D53 promoter and activates D53 transcription (negative feedback loop with D53 protein repressing IPA1)

evidence.title: IPA1 functions as a downstream TF repressed by D53
evidence.evidence_sentence: IPA1 directly binds D53 promoter GTAC element and activates D53 transcription, forming negative feedback loop.

edges.mechanism: IPA1 directly binds OsPIN1b promoter to activate auxin efflux carrier expression

evidence.title: Genome-Wide Binding Analysis of IPA1
evidence.evidence_sentence: EMSA confirmed IPA1 could directly target PIN1b promoter.

edges.mechanism: OsmiR156 cleaves IPA1/OsSPL14 mRNA via miR156 recognition site in 3' region

evidence.title: Regulation of OsSPL14 by OsmiR156 defines ideal plant architecture in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: One nucleotide substitution in the OsmiR156 recognition site perturbs OsmiR156-directed mRNA degradation; accumulation of OsSPL14 leads to ideal plant architecture.
evidence.claim: miR156 represses OsSPL14 by mRNA cleavage

edges.mechanism: IPI1 is a RING-type E3 ubiquitin ligase that ubiquitinates IPA1; K48-linked chains in panicle drive degradation, K63 chains in shoot apex are non-proteolytic

evidence.title: Tissue-Specific Ubiquitination by IPA1 INTERACTING PROTEIN1 Modulates IPA1 Protein Levels
evidence.evidence_sentence: IPI1 could promote targeting of the IPA1 complex with different polyubiquitin chains, and the diverse fate of IPA1 proteins could in turn regulate downstream genes; IPI1 promotes degradation of IPA1 in panicles.

edges.mechanism: OsSHI1 physically interacts with IPA1 and reduces IPA1 DNA binding affinity, inhibiting transcriptional activation activity

evidence.title: OsSHI1 Regulates Plant Architecture Through Modulating the Transcriptional Activity of IPA1
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsSHI1 repressed the transcriptional activation activity of IPA1 via physical interaction.

edges.mechanism: D27 is iron-containing beta-carotene isomerase, first step of SL biosynthesis converting all-trans to 9-cis-beta-carotene

evidence.title: DWARF27, an Iron-Containing Protein Required for the Biosynthesis of Strigolactones
evidence.evidence_sentence: 2'-epi-5-deoxystrigol was undetectable in d27 vs. wild-type root exudates.
evidence.claim: D27 is required for SL biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: D10 encodes CAROTENOID CLEAVAGE DIOXYGENASE 8 (CCD8) cleaving 9-cis-beta-apo-10'-carotenal to carlactone

evidence.title: DWARF10, an RMS1/MAX4/DAD1 ortholog, controls lateral bud outgrowth in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: D10 encodes a CAROTENOID CLEAVAGE DIOXYGENASE 8 (CCD8) involved in strigolactone biosynthesis.

edges.mechanism: D17/HTD1 encodes CCD7 catalyzing cleavage of 9-cis-beta-carotene to 9-cis-beta-apo-10'-carotenal

evidence.title: Genetic basis controlling rice plant architecture
evidence.evidence_sentence: D17/HIGH-TILLERING DWARF1 (HTD1) acts as enzyme in SL biosynthesis; rice D17 encodes CCD7.

edges.mechanism: Rice MAX1 homologs (Os900, Os1400, Os1500, Os1900, Os5100) convert carlactone to 4-deoxyorobanchol

evidence.title: Diverse Roles of MAX1 Homologues in Rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: Rice MAX1 (Os900) and other homologs convert carlactone to 4-deoxyorobanchol (4DO).
evidence.claim: OsMAX1 converts carlactone to canonical SLs

edges.mechanism: Auxin signaling via AXR1-TIR1 pathway induces D10 expression; positive feedback from apical auxin to SL biosynthesis

evidence.title: DWARF10 controls lateral bud outgrowth in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: Exogenous auxin up-regulates D10 in shoots.

evidence.title: Cytokinin and strigolactone signaling at the bud site
evidence.evidence_sentence: Auxin up-regulates D17/HTD1 and D10 via AXR1-TIR1 pathway.

edges.mechanism: Auxin induces D17/HTD1 CCD7 expression (parallel to D10 regulation)

evidence.title: Cytokinin and strigolactone signaling at the bud site
evidence.evidence_sentence: Auxin induces expression of D17 SL biosynthesis gene.

edges.mechanism: OsTB1/FC1 is a TCP family transcription factor expressed in axillary buds that inhibits bud outgrowth; key branching integrator downstream of SL

evidence.title: The OsTB1 gene negatively regulates lateral branching in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsTB1 functions as a negative regulator for lateral branching in rice.

evidence.title: FINE CULM1 (FC1) Works Downstream of Strigolactones
evidence.evidence_sentence: fc1 mutants show reduced plant height and increases in tiller numbers.
evidence.claim: Loss of OsTB1/FC1 increases tiller number

edges.mechanism: OsMADS57 binds CArG motif in D14 promoter and directly suppresses D14 transcription

evidence.title: OsMADS57-OsTB1 interaction modulates rice tillering via DWARF14
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsMADS57 bound to the CArG motif in the promoter and directly suppressed D14 expression.

edges.mechanism: miR444a cleaves OsMADS57 mRNA

evidence.title: OsMADS57-OsTB1 interaction modulates rice tillering via DWARF14
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsMADS57 gene is targeted and regulated negatively by miR444a.

edges.mechanism: MOC1 is a GRAS-family transcription factor essential for axillary meristem initiation and bud outgrowth

evidence.title: Control of tillering in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: MOC1 plays an essential role in the initiation of axillary bud formation; loss-of-function mutant moc1 exhibits a monoculm phenotype without tillers.
evidence.claim: MOC1 is required for tiller formation

evidence.title: SLR1 inhibits MOC1 degradation
evidence.evidence_sentence: MOC1 is required for the formation of axillary meristems and subsequent bud outgrowth.

edges.mechanism: TAD1 is Cdh1-type APC/C co-activator; recruits MOC1 to APC/C E3 ligase for cell-cycle-dependent ubiquitination and proteasomal degradation

evidence.title: Degradation of MONOCULM 1 by APC/CTAD1 regulates rice tillering
evidence.evidence_sentence: TAD1 interacts directly with MOC1; recruits MOC1 to APC/C and degrades MOC1 in cell-cycle-dependent manner; MOC1 accumulated to a higher level in tad1.

edges.mechanism: TE is CDC20-type APC/C co-activator; mediates ubiquitin-26S proteasome degradation of MOC1

evidence.title: Rice APC/CTE controls tillering by mediating the degradation of MONOCULM 1
evidence.evidence_sentence: TE mediates the degradation of MOC1 by ubiquitin-26S proteasome pathway; te mutant drastically increased tiller number.

edges.mechanism: SLR1 DELLA protein physically binds MOC1 and protects it from APC/C-mediated degradation

evidence.title: SLR1 inhibits MOC1 degradation
evidence.evidence_sentence: MOC1 is protected from degradation by binding to the DELLA protein SLR1; cell-free protein degradation assay showed degradation of MBP-MOC1 was inhibited by His-Trx-SLR1.

edges.mechanism: GA binds GID1 receptor, which promotes ubiquitin-mediated degradation of DELLA SLR1 via SCF-GID2 F-box

evidence.title: SLR1 inhibits MOC1 degradation
evidence.evidence_sentence: GAs trigger the degradation of SLR1, leading to stem elongation.

edges.mechanism: GA-GID1 complex targets NGR5 (AP2-TF) for proteasomal degradation

evidence.title: Enhanced sustainable green revolution yield via nitrogen-responsive chromatin modulation in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: GA receptor GID1 targets NGR5 for proteolysis by the 26S proteasome.

edges.mechanism: SLR1 competes with NGR5 for GID1 binding, preventing NGR5 ubiquitination and degradation

evidence.title: Enhanced sustainable green revolution yield
evidence.evidence_sentence: SLR1 competes with NGR5 for GID1 and prevents NGR5 from GA-mediated proteolysis.

edges.mechanism: NGR5 recruits PRC2 to OsTB1 promoter depositing H3K27me3 repressive mark

evidence.title: Enhanced sustainable green revolution yield
evidence.evidence_sentence: NGR5 directly interacts with PRC2 to form a repressive complex at the shoot branching inhibitory genes including OsTB1 in a nitrogen-dependent way.

edges.mechanism: NGR5-PRC2 complex represses D14 via H3K27me3

evidence.title: Enhanced sustainable green revolution yield
evidence.evidence_sentence: NGR5 recruits PRC2 to branching inhibitory genes such as D14 for H3K27me3 repression.

edges.mechanism: Nitrogen supply (nitrate sensing via NRT1.1B) promotes NGR5 accumulation and PRC2 recruitment

evidence.title: Enhanced sustainable green revolution yield
evidence.evidence_sentence: Nitrogen supply promotes tillering via NGR5 by chromatin modulation at branching-inhibitor genes.

edges.mechanism: OsTCP19 TCP TF directly binds DLT promoter and represses its transcription; inhibits N-responsive tillering

evidence.title: Genomic basis of geographical adaptation to soil nitrogen in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsTCP19 directly represses the expression of DWARF AND LOW TILLERING (DLT); N-induced LBD37/LBD39 directly inhibit OsTCP19.

edges.mechanism: DLT is GRAS-family BR signaling TF promoting tiller outgrowth

evidence.title: DLT, GRAS family BR signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: DLT (DWARF AND LOW TILLERING) plays positive roles in BR signaling and tiller outgrowth.

edges.mechanism: N-induced OsLBD37 represses OsTCP19 transcription, relieving DLT repression and enabling N-induced tillering

evidence.title: Genomic basis of geographical adaptation to soil nitrogen
evidence.evidence_sentence: Nitrogen-induced LATERAL ORGAN BOUNDARIES DOMAIN proteins OsLBD37 and OsLBD39 can directly repress OsTCP19.

edges.mechanism: High nitrogen induces OsLBD37 transcription

evidence.title: Genomic basis of geographical adaptation to soil nitrogen
evidence.evidence_sentence: Nitrogen induces OsLBD37/39 which then repress OsTCP19.

edges.mechanism: OsCKX2/Gn1a irreversibly degrades cytokinin (N6-isopentenyladenine, trans-zeatin)

evidence.title: Cytokinin oxidase regulates rice grain production
evidence.evidence_sentence: Gn1a encodes cytokinin oxidase/dehydrogenase (OsCKX2); reduced expression causes cytokinin accumulation in inflorescence meristems.

edges.mechanism: OsCKX9 degrades cytokinin; nuclear/cytosolic dual-localized enzyme

evidence.title: Strigolactone promotes cytokinin degradation through transcriptional activation of CYTOKININ OXIDASE/DEHYDROGENASE 9
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsCKX9 functions as a cytosolic and nuclear dual-localized CK catabolic enzyme.

edges.mechanism: BAP cytokinin suppresses OsTB1/FC1 expression in a dose-dependent manner; independent of SL pathway

evidence.title: FINE CULM1 (FC1) Works Downstream of Strigolactones
evidence.evidence_sentence: BAP treatment clearly suppressed FC1 expression in a concentration-dependent manner; negative control of FC1 expression by cytokinin is independent of the SL pathway.

edges.mechanism: OsIPT genes (especially OsIPT4, OsIPT7) catalyze rate-limiting step of cytokinin biosynthesis (adenylate isopentenyltransferase)

evidence.title: Characterization of Genes Involved in Cytokinin Signaling and Metabolism from Rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: Of eight IPTs implicated in cytokinin biosynthesis, OsIPT4 and OsIPT7 are major contributors in roots and shoots.

edges.mechanism: Apical auxin signaling represses IPT genes in nodes, reducing cytokinin biosynthesis; apical dominance mechanism

evidence.title: Auxin in axillary bud inhibition through repression of cytokinin biosynthesis
evidence.evidence_sentence: Auxin down-regulates the expression of pea IPT family genes through AXR-TIR-dependent auxin signaling pathway.

edges.mechanism: OsLOG converts inactive cytokinin nucleotides to active free-base form

evidence.title: Characterization of Genes Involved in Cytokinin Signaling and Metabolism from Rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsLOG converts cytokinin nucleotides to active free bases; multiple OsLOG genes expressed with tissue specificity.

edges.mechanism: CK binds OsHK4 histidine kinase CHASE domain activating phosphorelay

evidence.title: Cytokinin receptor OHK4/OsHK4 regulates inflorescence architecture
evidence.evidence_sentence: Cytokinin binds OsHK4 histidine kinase receptor activating downstream phosphorelay; D86-Q87 deletion abolishes receptor dimerization and CK binding.

edges.mechanism: Activated OsHK4 phosphorylates OsAHP1/2 histidine phosphotransfer proteins

evidence.title: Characterization of Genes Involved in Cytokinin Signaling and Metabolism
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsAHP1/2 are downstream phosphotransfer proteins in rice CK signaling connecting OsHKs to response regulators.

edges.mechanism: OsAHP phosphorelay activates type-B OsRR21 response regulator for target gene transcription

evidence.title: OHK4/OsHK4 regulates inflorescence via IPA1 feedback
evidence.evidence_sentence: The response of OsRR21 phosphorylation to CK was diminished in the pal1 mutant; OsAHP mediates this relay.

edges.mechanism: Type-B OsRR21 directly binds CK response elements in IPA1 promoter activating transcription

evidence.title: OHK4/OsHK4 regulates inflorescence via IPA1 feedback
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsRR21 directly activates IPA1 by binding to 10 CK response elements in IPA1 promoter (Y1H, ChIP-qPCR validated).

edges.mechanism: OsPIN1b auxin efflux transporter, knockout shows increased tillers

evidence.title: OsPIN1b mutants show increased tillers
evidence.evidence_sentence: Knockout mutants of OsPIN1b increase tiller numbers.

edges.mechanism: OsPIN9 ammonium-induced auxin efflux transporter promotes tiller bud outgrowth

evidence.title: OsPIN9 regulates tillering
evidence.evidence_sentence: Overexpression of OsPIN9 result in increased tillering; knockout result in decreased tillering.

edges.mechanism: Ammonium specifically induces OsPIN9 expression (not nitrate)

evidence.title: OsPIN9 regulates tillering
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsPIN9 expression is induced by ammonium, a major source of nitrogen.

edges.mechanism: Apical auxin signaling activates OsTB1/FC1 expression in axillary buds (conserved with Arabidopsis BRC1)

evidence.title: Auxin-induced apical dominance requires BRC1
evidence.evidence_sentence: Auxin-induced apical dominance requires the activity of BRC1; same mechanism in rice via OsTB1/FC1.

edges.mechanism: OsCCA1 circadian MYB-like TF binds CBS element in OsTB1 promoter

evidence.title: Rice Circadian Clock Regulates Tiller Growth
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsCCA1 directly binds CBS elements in OsTB1 promoter activating expression.

edges.mechanism: OsCCA1 directly binds CBS in D14 promoter

evidence.title: Rice Circadian Clock Regulates Tiller Growth
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsCCA1 directly binds CBS in D14 promoter; ChIP-qPCR confirmed.

edges.mechanism: OsCCA1 binds CBS in D10 promoter

evidence.title: Rice Circadian Clock Regulates Tiller Growth
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsCCA1 directly binds CBS in D10 promoter.

edges.mechanism: OsCCA1 binds CBS/EE elements in IPA1 promoter

evidence.title: Rice Circadian Clock Regulates Tiller Growth
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsCCA1 directly binds CBS/EE elements in IPA1 promoter.

edges.mechanism: OsPRR1 pseudo-response regulator represses OsCCA1 in reciprocal feedback loop

evidence.title: Rice Circadian Clock Regulates Tiller Growth
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsPRR1 forms reciprocal feedback loop with OsCCA1 (negative repression).

edges.mechanism: OsCCA1 binds CBS in OsPRR1 promoter and represses expression (part of evening loop)

evidence.title: Rice Circadian Clock Regulates Tiller Growth
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsCCA1 directly binds CBS on OsPRR1 promoter (negative regulation in reciprocal feedback).

edges.mechanism: Sucrose signaling stabilizes D53 protein (antagonizes SL-induced degradation)

evidence.title: Sucrose promotes D53 accumulation and tillering in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: Sucrose promotes D53 accumulation and tillering in rice; antagonizes SL-mediated D53 degradation.

edges.mechanism: Photosynthetic sugars downregulate OsCCA1 in roots and buds (tissue-dependent)

evidence.title: Rice Circadian Clock Regulates Tiller Growth
evidence.evidence_sentence: Photosynthetic sugars negatively regulate OsCCA1 expression in roots/buds.

edges.mechanism: MOC2 encodes fructose-1,6-bisphosphatase1 for sucrose biosynthesis pathway

evidence.title: MOC2 encodes FBP1 in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: MOC2 encodes a fructose-1,6-bisphosphatase1 responsible for the sucrose biosynthesis pathway.

edges.mechanism: LAX1 bHLH TF required for axillary meristem initiation; non-cell-autonomous movement essential

evidence.title: Two-Step Regulation of LAX PANICLE1 Protein Accumulation
evidence.evidence_sentence: LAX1 is required for AM initiation throughout plant's lifetime; lax1 mutants fail to progress into AM formation.

edges.mechanism: FON2 CLV3-like peptide restricts TAB1 expression domain via CLAVATA-like receptor signaling

evidence.title: Antagonistic action of TAB1 and FON2
evidence.evidence_sentence: FON2 restricts the stem cell population by moderately repressing TAB1 expression.

edges.mechanism: MOC3 (TAB1/OsWUS) physically interacts with MOC1 to activate FON1

evidence.title: Potential of rice tillering
evidence.evidence_sentence: Physical interaction between MOC1 and MOC3 can up-regulate the expression of FON1.

edges.mechanism: FON1 (CLAVATA1 ortholog) specifically affects tiller bud elongation; fon1 forms buds but fails to elongate

evidence.title: Potential of rice tillering
evidence.evidence_sentence: fon1 mutant forms them normally but fails to elongate tiller buds.

edges.mechanism: OsBZR1 recruits D53 to OsTB1 promoter repressing transcription; D53-BZR1 complex under BR signaling

evidence.title: SLs and BRs antagonistically regulate D53-OsBZR1 complex
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsBZR1 recruits D53 to repress the expression of OsTB1 forming D53-BZR1 complex.

edges.mechanism: BR stabilizes OsBZR1 via inhibition of OsBIN2 kinase (dephosphorylated BZR1)

evidence.title: SLs and BRs antagonistically regulate D53-OsBZR1 complex
evidence.evidence_sentence: BR is known to stabilize the OsBZR1 protein.

edges.mechanism: ABA promotes bud dormancy

evidence.title: Potential of rice tillering
evidence.evidence_sentence: ABA involved in axillary bud dormancy and suppression of outgrowth.

edges.mechanism: DST zinc finger protein binds DBS motif in OsCKX2 promoter and activates transcription; reg1 allele loses activation but retains binding

evidence.title: Rice zinc finger DST controls Gn1a/OsCKX2
evidence.evidence_sentence: ChIP assay confirms DST binds DBS motif in OsCKX2 promoter; DST-REG1 increases luciferase activity driven by OsCKX2 promoter.

edges.mechanism: P-sufficient conditions repress SL biosynthesis genes D27, D10, D17; P-deficiency elevates them

evidence.title: Contribution of strigolactones to tiller bud inhibition under phosphate deficiency
evidence.evidence_sentence: Under P-deficient conditions, expression levels of SL biosynthesis genes were elevated; P-deficiency elevates SL content.

edges.mechanism: P deficiency elevates D10/CCD8 expression

evidence.title: SL under phosphate deficiency
evidence.evidence_sentence: P deficiency elevates D10 expression driving SL biosynthesis.

edges.mechanism: P deficiency elevates D17 expression

evidence.title: SL under phosphate deficiency
evidence.evidence_sentence: P deficiency elevates D17 expression.

edges.mechanism: Decapitation removes apical auxin source, releasing axillary buds from apical dominance

evidence.title: Sugar demand initial regulator of apical dominance
evidence.evidence_sentence: Decapitation removes apical auxin source; rapid sugar flux precedes auxin depletion at buds.

edges.mechanism: N sufficiency represses SL biosynthesis genes D10, D17, D27

evidence.title: Strigolactones and nitrogen in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: N-deficient conditions upregulate D10, D17, D27; high N represses them.

edges.mechanism: N sufficiency represses D17/HTD1 SL biosynthesis

evidence.title: Strigolactones and nitrogen in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: N-deficient conditions upregulate D17; high N represses.

edges.mechanism: N sufficiency represses D27 SL biosynthesis

evidence.title: Strigolactones and nitrogen in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: N-deficient conditions upregulate D27; high N represses.

edges.mechanism: Light signal entrains OsCCA1 expression (canonical circadian clock input)

evidence.title: Rice Circadian Clock Regulates Tiller Growth
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsCCA1 shows morning-peak circadian expression entrained by light, and integrates sugar signaling in tiller regulation.

edges.mechanism: Photoperiod regulates OsPRR1 evening expression

evidence.title: Rice Circadian Clock Regulates Tiller Growth
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsPRR1 evening-peak expression is photoperiod-dependent in rice clock.

edges.mechanism: OsTAR2 is TAA/TAR aminotransferase; first step of IAA biosynthesis from tryptophan to IPA

evidence.title: Advances in rice auxin
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsTAR2 catalyzes the first step of tryptophan-dependent IAA biosynthesis in rice.

edges.mechanism: OsYUCCA enzymes catalyze rate-limiting step of IAA biosynthesis (IPA to IAA)

evidence.title: Advances in rice auxin
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsYUCCAs are rate-limiting enzymes in auxin synthesis; multiple members contribute to tiller angle and architecture.

edges.mechanism: MOC1 positively affects axillary meristem gene network including LAX1

evidence.title: LAX PANICLE1 regulation
evidence.evidence_sentence: MOC1 works together with LAX1 in axillary meristem formation; lax1 moc1 double mutant shows enhanced AM suppression.

edges.mechanism: Sucrose synthesis generates trehalose-6-phosphate (T6P) signaling molecule

evidence.title: Branching sucrose and SL signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: Mounting evidence indicates that sucrose effects on branching are mediated by trehalose-6-phosphate (T6P).

edges.mechanism: T6P signaling represses BRC1/OsTB1 expression

evidence.title: Sucrose SL signaling bud outgrowth
evidence.evidence_sentence: Sucrose/T6P signaling partially represses PsBRC1 (OsTB1 ortholog) expression in peripheral buds.

edges.mechanism: NRT1.1B-dependent phosphorylation promotes NGR5 stability (nitrate-sensing-to-chromatin)

evidence.title: NRT1.1B natural variation
evidence.evidence_sentence: NRT1.1B natural variation in indica enhances N uptake signaling that converges on NGR5 activity.

edges.mechanism: OsGA20ox2 (SD1) catalyzes multiple oxidation steps in GA biosynthesis

evidence.title: Green revolution mutant rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: sd1 allele is a null mutation in GA20ox2; sd1 plants have reduced GA levels.

edges.mechanism: CK signaling induces type-A OsRRs as primary response genes (negative feedback on CK signaling)

evidence.title: Rice cytokinin signaling genes
evidence.evidence_sentence: Most type-A OsRR genes rapidly upregulated by cytokinin (peak 30-60 min).

edges.mechanism: Type-A OsRRs provide negative feedback on CK signaling

evidence.title: Rice cytokinin signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: Type-A OsRRs act as negative regulators of CK signaling (classical feedback).

edges.mechanism: BR signaling activates DLT expression/function in tiller promotion

evidence.title: DLT in BR signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: DLT plays positive roles in brassinosteroid signaling in rice; BR pathway activates DLT.

edges.mechanism: NGR5 binds IPA1/OsSPL14 promoter and recruits PRC2 complex to deposit H3K27me3 mark; ChIP-qPCR direct binding confirmed; nitrogen dose-dependent

evidence.title: Enhanced sustainable green revolution yield via nitrogen-responsive chromatin modulation in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: ChIP-qPCR analysis showed that NGR5 can directly bind to the promoter region of D14 and SPL14, and the binding strength increased with increasing nitrogen concentration.

edges.mechanism: Photosynthesis-derived sucrose activates NGR5 expression at the shoot base, providing carbon-nitrogen integration for tiller bud elongation

evidence.title: Photosynthesis regulates tillering bud elongation and nitrogen-use efficiency via sugar-induced NGR5 in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: The expression of nitrogen-mediated tiller growth response 5 (NGR5) was remarkably decreased in shoot base of dtn1-2, which can be activated by sucrose treatment.

edges.mechanism: DTN1 (Decreased Tiller Number 1) encodes fructose-1,6-bisphosphate aldolase and is required for photosynthesis and sucrose accumulation in shoot base / axillary buds

evidence.title: Photosynthesis regulates tillering bud elongation and nitrogen-use efficiency via sugar-induced NGR5 in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: Deficiency of DTN1 results in reduced photosynthetic rate and decreased contents of sucrose and other sugars in both leaves and axillary buds.

edges.mechanism: OsVIL2 chromatin-interacting factor binds OsTB1 promoter and represses OsTB1 expression via chromatin modification, releasing axillary bud outgrowth

evidence.title: Chromatin Interacting Factor OsVIL2 Is Required for Outgrowth of Axillary Buds in Rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: Expression of OsTB1 was increased in osvil2 mutants. ChIP assays showed that OsVIL2 binds to the promoter region of OsTB1 in wild-type rice; tiller number of double mutant osvil2 ostb1 was similar to that of ostb1 (osvil2 epistatic to ostb1).

edges.mechanism: OsGATA8 transcription factor represses OsTCP19 (negative regulator of tillering), promoting productive tiller formation under N-replete conditions

evidence.title: The elite haplotype OsGATA8-H coordinates nitrogen uptake and productive tiller formation in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsGATA8 promotes tiller formation by repressing the transcription of OsTCP19, a negative modulator of tillering.

edges.mechanism: Os1900 (one of five rice MAX1-like paralogs) catalyzes carlactone-to-carlactonoic acid conversion together with Os5100; Os1900 is the N-fertilization-responsive paralog

evidence.title: Fertilization controls tiller numbers via transcriptional regulation of a MAX1-like gene in rice cultivation
evidence.evidence_sentence: Os1900 together with another MAX1-like gene, Os5100, play a critical role in controlling the conversion of carlactone into carlactonoic acid during strigolactone biosynthesis and tillering in rice.

edges.mechanism: Fertilization (N supply) transcriptionally upregulates Os1900 promoter; promoter mutations that disrupt N-induction increase tiller numbers under low fertilizer

evidence.title: Fertilization controls tiller numbers via transcriptional regulation of a MAX1-like gene in rice cultivation
evidence.evidence_sentence: Fertilization controls tiller number in rice through transcriptional regulation of Os1900, and a few promoter mutations alone can increase tiller numbers and grain yields even under minor-fertilizer conditions.

edges.mechanism: MOC1 physically interacts with MOC3 (OsWUS/TAB1) and acts as a transcriptional co-activator; MOC1 cannot bind FON1 promoter alone but cooperates with MOC3 binding

evidence.title: Tiller Bud Formation Regulators MOC1 and MOC3 Cooperatively Promote Tiller Bud Outgrowth by Activating FON1 Expression in Rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: MOC3 is able to directly bind the promoter of FON1 and subsequently activate FON1 expression, while MOC1 functions as a co-activator of MOC3 but could not directly bind the FON1 promoter.
evidence.claim: MOC1 cooperates with MOC3 as co-activator

edges.mechanism: T20 encodes ζ-carotene isomerase (Z-ISO) acting upstream of D27 in the carotenoid pathway; required for SL precursor supply

evidence.title: ζ-Carotene Isomerase Suppresses Tillering in Rice through the Coordinated Biosynthesis of Strigolactone and Abscisic Acid
evidence.evidence_sentence: The T20 gene encodes a chloroplast ζ-carotene isomerase (Z-ISO), which is involved in the biosynthesis of carotenoids and their metabolites, strigolactone (SL) and abscisic acid (ABA).
